message:
feat(tui): restore confirmation dialogs for destructive worker actions

Re-implement the confirmation dialog feature that was lost during a
rebase conflict. This feature prevents accidental execution of
destructive operations in the TUI dashboard.

The feature was originally committed as 217c424 but was skipped during
rebase due to conflicts with concurrent TUI widget refactoring. This
commit reconstructs the feature by carefully merging it with the
current codebase.

New types and infrastructure:
- ConfirmAction enum: DrainWorker, EnableWorker, CancelBuild, KillBuild
- ConfirmationDialog struct: tracks action and yes/no selection state
- InputMode enum: Normal, TextInput, Confirm for context-aware keybindings
- status.rs module: centralized status indicator mappings for TUI

Event handling changes (event.rs):
- Added poll_event() function that accepts InputMode parameter
- Added ConfirmYes (y/Y) and ConfirmNo (n/N/Esc) actions
- Added CancelBuild (c) and KillBuild (K) keybindings
- handle_key_confirm_mode() for dialog-specific input handling
- Legacy poll_event_with_mode() now delegates to poll_event()

Application logic changes (app.rs):
- get_input_mode(): determines current mode from state
- execute_confirmed_action(): async handler for confirmed operations
- Confirmation dialogs take precedence over other UI overlays
- Tab/arrows toggle yes/no selection in dialog mode
- Enter executes based on current selection
- Escape/q/n cancels dialog without executing

State changes (state.rs):
- Added confirmation_dialog field to TuiState
- Added selected_active_build() helper for build panel selection
- ConfirmAction provides description() and context() for display

Widget changes (widgets.rs):
- render_confirmation_dialog(): centered modal with action description
- Dialog shows context about what the action will do
- Visual highlight on selected yes/no button using color scheme

Safety features:
- Default selection is "No" for all destructive actions
- Clear visual feedback on which option is selected
- Context text explains the effect of each action
- Escape key always cancels without executing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
